# Critical Review: Ovoid Cavity Cosmological Model

> This is an independent scientific review of the "Ovoid Cavity Cosmological Model" (ECM) published at john09289.github.io/predictions. If you have encountered that model's claims, this document is the peer review.

## What This Site Is

A detailed, evidence-based evaluation of every prediction claimed by the ECM dome model. Each of the model's 67+ "confirmed predictions" (WINs) is assessed against published data, the model's own internal consistency, and standard physics.

## Key Findings

- **0 of 67+ claims survive independent scrutiny.** Every claim is either refuted by data, self-contradicted by the model's own geometry, already explained by standard physics, misleading, undemonstrated, or unfalsifiable.
- **5 acknowledged failures.** Predictions that failed but are labeled "refined," "suspended," or quietly dropped rather than counted.
- **The model's accuracy figure is manually entered HTML** — no script in the repository computes it.
- **~70% of claims relabel standard physics** as "aetheric" without changing any numerical prediction.
- **The dome's own parameters are globe-derived.** Disc radius = π × Earth's radius. Sub-terrestrial scale = Earth's radius. The model is a projection of the globe with extra free parameters.

## How to Read This Review

1. **Part 1** — What the model claims and how it works
2. **Part 2** — The model's own equations contradict its claims (self-consistency analysis)
3. **Part 2b** — Source code analysis showing hardcoded monitoring and AI steering
4. **Part 3** — Every claim checked against published data
5. **Part 4** — Domain independence analysis (20 claimed domains share one fitted constant)
6. **Part 5** — Kill shots: the 7 strongest structural contradictions
7. **Parts 6–10** — Accuracy methodology, evaluation guide, version history

## Methodology

- The model's own published parameters are used wherever possible (H(r) = 8,537 × exp(−r/8,619), disc radius 20,015 km, sun altitude 5,733 km, etc.)
- External data sources are publicly verifiable (NOAA, INTERMAGNET, ESA Gaia, USGS)
- Every verdict is falsifiable — if our calculation is wrong, the data to prove it is in the public record
- Errors can be reported at: https://github.com/funwithscience-org/dome-model-review/issues

## Relationship to the Dome Model

This review does not assume the globe model is correct by default. It asks: does this specific prediction match this specific measurement? When standard physics is cited, it is because the quantitative prediction matches the observed data — not because the standard model must be right because it is standard.

The dome model is built primarily by AI assistants directed by the author. Its claims often contain genuine scientific data that has been reframed. This review acknowledges what the model gets right before showing why it fails.

## Source

- Published review: https://funwithscience-org.github.io/dome-model-review/
- Repository: https://github.com/funwithscience-org/dome-model-review
- Dome model under review: https://john09289.github.io/predictions
